flows: utente -> cliente - type: association; label: "eredita from"; explanation: "eredita from"
cliente -> venditore - type: association; label: "eredita from"; explanation: "eredita from"
venditore -> venditorepreferito - type: association; label: "venditorepreferito"; explanation: "venditorepreferito"
flow 1: utente -> cliente -> venditore -> venditorepreferito
the diagram models a system where users can create accounts and interact with clients and sellers.
the system has a preference feature that allows users to select their preferred seller.
the system sends notifications to users based on their interactions with clients and sellers.
the system has a login feature that allows users to log in with their email and password.
client -> server — type=association; label="login"; explanation="client requests login"
server -> database — type=association; label="get user"; explanation="server retrieves user data"
client -> server — type=association; label="logout"; explanation="client requests logout"
server -> database — type=association; label="create user"; explanation="server creates new user"
flow 1: client -> server -> database -> client -> server -> client
flow 2: none

the diagram models a simple login process where the client requests login, the server checks the credentials, and if successful, returns the user's data. if the login fails, the server returns an error message.
the client can then choose to logout or continue using the system.
the server and database are separate entities that interact with each other and the client.
flow 1: click su un prodotto -> modifica prodotto -> premuto tasto "modifica" -> i campi sono compilati correttamente? -> no -> segnala errore
flow 2: i campi sono compilati correttamente? -> si -> applica modifiche prodotto
conditions/branches:
i campi sono compilati correttamente?: if the fields are correctly filled, then apply product modifications; otherwise, signal an error
loops/repetitions:
notes/annotations:
the diagram models a sequence of actions and decisions related to modifying a product.
the main flows involve clicking on a product, modifying it, and applying the modifications.
the decision point checks if the fields are correctly filled, and if so, applies the modifications; otherwise, it signals an error.
flow 1: click su pulsante -> aggiungi prodotto -> premuto tasto "aggiungi" -> i campi sono compilati correttamente? -> aggiungi prodotto
i campi sono compilati correttamente? -> si: if fields are filled correctly, the product is added; no: if fields are not filled correctly, an error is indicated

the diagram models a sequence of actions where a user clicks a button, adds a product, presses the "add" button, checks if fields are filled correctly, and either adds the product or indicates an error.
the main flow is a linear sequence of actions.
there are no loops or repetitions in the diagram.
the diagram does not show any error handling or alternative paths.
click su -> gestione notifiche — type=trigger; label="click su"; explanation="clicking the button triggers the notification management process"
gestione notifiche -> premuto "push notification" — type=flow; label="gestione notifiche"; explanation="the notification management process triggers the push notification"
premuto "push notification" -> invia notifiche in app — type=flow; label="premuto 'push notification'"; explanation="the push notification triggers the push of notifications to the app"
premuto "in app notification" -> invia notifiche in app — type=flow; label="premuto 'in app notification'"; explanation="the in-app notification triggers the push of notifications to the app"
flow 1: click su -> gestione notifiche -> premuto "push notification" -> invia notifiche in app -> premuto "in app notification" -> invia notifiche in app
the diagram models the process of managing notifications, from clicking a button to pushing notifications to an app.
the main flow involves the sequence of events from clicking the button to pushing notifications to the app.
there are no decisions or branches in the flow.
the diagram does not show any loops or repeated behavior.
user -> new product — type=association; label="push"; explanation="push notification"
new product -> app — type=association; label="inappnotification"; explanation="in-app notification"
flow 1: user -> new product -> app
the diagram models the interaction between a user and a new product, where the user can either push or receive in-app notifications.
the main flow is a simple sequence of events where the user interacts with the new product, which then sends a push or in-app notification.
there are no loops or repeated behavior in the diagram.
validations: if login fails: server -> client — type=association; label="login failed"; explanation="server returns error message"
if login succeeds: server -> client — type=association; label="login success"; explanation="server returns user data"
new product has a decision node with two branches: "push" and "in app notification"
